---
title: "Programming"
author: "Nico Steckhan"
output: html_document
---

I program mainly in R and Python for research computing, data science, and digital health applications. Most of my work centers on turning complex real-world data into analysis pipelines, models, and usable prototypes.

## What I build

- Statistical and machine learning workflows for clinical, behavioral, and physiological data
- Pipelines for multimodal sensor data, including wearables, continuous glucose monitoring, and IoT streams
- Feature engineering, data augmentation, and sensor fusion workflows
- Proof-of-concept systems for digital health and connected sensing environments

## Technical focus

- R for statistical modeling, analysis, visualization, and reporting
- Python for machine learning, data processing, and rapid prototyping
- TensorFlow and Keras for applied modeling
- Linux, virtual machines, and research infrastructure for reproducible work
- High-performance computing when projects need scale

## Current interests

- Digital phenotyping
- Wearable sensing in real-world settings
- Interpretable machine learning for health applications
- Sensor-software integration across IoT and IoHT systems

Code and experiments are available on [GitHub](https://github.com/steckhan).
